claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 10 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 19 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 8 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I'm inquiring about my three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I need to understand more about each of your children to assess eligibility.

Let's start with Alex - how old is Alex?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And how old is Blake?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And how old is Charlie?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about Blake specifically, since they are 19 years old. Is Blake still in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, yes. Alex and Blake both live with me at home, but Charlie doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Charlie doesn't live with you, I need to understand your situation better. Are you paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards Charlie's upkeep (for example, on food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children.

Let me evaluate each child's situation:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, here's the outcome for your Child Benefit eligibility:

**Alex (10 years old): ✓ ELIGIBLE**
You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is under 16 and lives with you in the UK.

**Blake (19 years old): ✗ NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake because, although Blake is under 20, Blake is not in approved education or training, which is a requirement for children aged 16-19.

**Charlie (2 years old): ✗ NOT ELIGIBLE**
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie because Charlie doesn't live with you and you're not contributing financially to Charlie's upkeep. To qualify when a child doesn't live with you, you need to be paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care.

**Summary:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex only. If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if Blake enters approved education or training, or if you start contributing to Charlie's upkeep), your eligibility may change.

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old and lives with you in the UK. Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Since Alex is under 16 and lives with you, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), but does not live with you and you are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep. Under the rules, you're usually responsible for a child if either you live with them OR you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. Since neither condition is met, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (10), Blake (19), and Charlie (2). The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and Blake, but Charlie lives elsewhere and the claimant makes no financial contributions. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only, as Alex is under 16 and lives with them. They are not eligible for Blake because Blake is 19 and not in approved education or training. They are not eligible for Charlie because Charlie doesn't live with them and they don't contribute to Charlie's upkeep.

Since Alex is under 16 and lives with you, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 19 years old and not in approved education or training. According to the rules, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Since Blake is not in approved education or training, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 2 years old (under 16), but does not live with you and you are not contributing financially towards Charlie's upkeep. Under the rules, you're usually responsible for a child if either you live with them OR you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. Since neither condition is met, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children: Alex (10), Blake (19), and Charlie (2). The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and Blake, but Charlie lives elsewhere and the claimant makes no financial contributions. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex only, as Alex is under 16 and lives with them. They are not eligible for Blake because Blake is 19 and not in approved education or training. They are not eligible for Charlie because Charlie doesn't live with them and they don't contribute to Charlie's upkeep.
